setting.time: timeless, in an age when dragons still shaped the sky
setting.place: the Stormspire Peaks, where mountains scrape the bellies of clouds
setting.mood: dreamy and warm, with the soft hum of wind through scales

characters.name: Veyra
characters.motivation: to preserve the beauty of the sky before mortals forget how to look up
characters.arc: from solitary collector to generous guardian who learns that clouds are meant to be released

characters.name: Pip
characters.motivation: to find the rain cloud that once raised him from an orphaned hatchling
characters.arc: from desperate searcher to someone who understands that letting go is its own kind of love

acts.name: The Hoard
acts.summary: Veyra's mountain lair is filled with bottled clouds — cumulus, nimbus, wisps of fog — each labeled and shelved like rare jewels.
scenes: Veyra arranges her newest acquisition: a perfect sunset-pink altocumulus, cradled in amber glass.
Pip arrives at the Stormspire, desperate, begging Veyra for a specific storm cloud she captured decades ago.

acts.name: The Storm
acts.summary: Veyra refuses, but Pip's plea stirs something — a memory of why she began collecting in the first place.
scenes: A flashback reveals young Veyra watching her first sunset cloud dissolve, heartbroken by its impermanence.
Pip argues that clouds lose their magic when locked away; they are alive only when free.
Veyra clutches her favorite cloud — a tiny thunderhead — and feels its faint, sad pulse.

acts.name: The Release
acts.summary: Veyra opens every jar. The sky erupts with color and rain and wonder.
scenes: She uncorks the amber jar; the sunset cloud unfurls across the evening sky, breathtaking.
One by one, clouds pour out — the mountain's caverns fill with wind and mist and roaring joy.
Pip finds his rain cloud among the released, recognizes its gentle rumble, and weeps.
Veyra watches the empty jars and smiles, realizing she never needed to own beauty — only witness it.
